name: rad-1password
description:
End-to-end 1Password CLI (`op`) coverage with a hardcoded-secrets scanner. Skills cover secret references (`op://...`), `op inject` / `op run` / `op read` for keeping secrets out of code and shell history, item CRUD with assignment statements and JSON templates, vault and user provisioning with granular permissions, SSH key generation and use, third-party CLI auth via `op plugin run`, and service-account / Connect-server patterns for CI/CD and headless servers. One router skill, ten topical sub-skills, four slash commands.

Bundled `scripts/scan-hardcoded-secrets.py` is a pure-stdlib Python scanner that finds provider-prefixed tokens (OpenAI, Anthropic, GitHub PAT, Stripe, AWS, Slack, Google, Twilio, SendGrid, Mailgun, Supabase JWT), URL-embedded passwords (postgres / mongodb / mysql / redis / https / etc.), and assignment-shaped credentials (`api_key = "..."`, `password: "..."`). For each finding, suggests a concrete `op://<vault>/<item>/<field>` reference shape for replacement. Skips `.env*` files, lockfiles, test fixtures, and obvious placeholders. Not a replacement for gitleaks/trufflehog — it's for in-editor use during development with explicit op:// migration suggestions. Cross-checked against `op` v2.34.0.
